column1: # The STANDING invariant (docs/CRYPTO_PERF.md): every C crypto primitive on the
# consensus path must stay AT LEAST as fast as its Rust/blst counterpart
# may only ever get FASTER. This file is the pinned line. It is consumed by
# the live C median (build/bin/zclassic23 -bench-crypto-vs-rust) and enforces:
#            c_ns_baseline is a CEILING — it may only shrink as we optimise.
#            A self-regression FAILS. margin default 0.20 (see the script).
#                                (we must stay ahead). Only valid when
#                                which makes the beat hard-fail FLAKE-PROOF:
#                                any run that passes the ratchet is < rust.
#            gate_mode=behind -> NO hard fail (would red main today); prints a
#                                loud "BEHIND RUST — optimize" line and relies
#                                on the ratchet for monotonic improvement.
#   rust_source        MEASURED | CITED  (+ where the Rust number comes from)
#   notes              provenance of the C baseline
# C baselines measured 2026-07-13 on AMD Ryzen 9 7950X3D (this host)
# pinned slightly above the max observed across runs for ratchet headroom.
# Rust numbers are CITED published reference points (blst / RustCrypto /
# librustzcash / zcashd) on comparable modern x86-64 unless labelled MEASURED —
# NO Rust is linked into the shipped binary (no-external-deps rule); this is a
# bench-only comparison. Re-baseline (shrink only) on this host as we optimise.

column5: CITED zcashd C++ CheckEquihashSolution ~150-175us (no Rust equihash exists; librustzcash predates it)
column6: check_equihash_solution on baked real 200,9 witness; obs 115.6-126.1us
fp_mul on canonical Fp fixtures; obs 56ns (pure C, mulx via -v3)
bls12_381_pairing on canonical G1/G2 generators; obs 1.79-1.87ms

column5: CITED RustCrypto k256 ecdsa verify ~85-95us (libsecp256k1 C bindings match ours)
column6: pubkey_verify on locally-signed 32-byte hash; obs 47.9-49.1us (vendored libsecp256k1)
ed25519_verify on RFC 8032 Test 2; obs 1.47-1.49ms (pure C, no windowing — OPTIMIZE)
sha256 over 1 KiB; obs 1.97-2.01us = ~0.52GB/s (no SHA-NI on -v3)
zcl_sha3_256 over 1 KiB; obs 1.71-1.78us = ~0.58GB/s (slim; establish margin)

column5: CITED librustzcash single Sapling output proof verify ~2-4ms
column6: sapling_check_output full BLS12-381 pairing on real prover proof; obs 7.56-7.92ms (BEHIND — primary optimize target)
